_comment: Desired-state branch policies for the protected branch — the Azure DevOps analogue of the GitHub ruleset (branch-protection.json). Consumed by scripts/configure-branch-policies.sh, which turns each entry into an `az repos policy` call. The check contexts here are Azure BUILD VALIDATION policies (one per gate pipeline), replacing GitHub required status checks; the approver-count + required-reviewer entries replace GitHub's required non-author approval + CODEOWNERS.

build_validation.note: CI — hard block. The mechanical gate.

OPTIONAL — keep only if ci.yml still contains the eval-gate stage AND you split it into its own pipeline, OR drop this entry and rely on the build-and-test policy (the eval-gate stage runs inside CI).

ADVISES. Blocking here only enforces 'the grader ran' — the grader pipeline always concludes success, so its verdict never blocks. Set isBlocking:false if you only want the advisory comment without gating on it having run.
BLOCKS on a high-confidence defect. Passes trivially when no source changed.
BLOCKS on HIGH. Passes trivially when no gated path changed and no risk:high label.
BLOCKS on degradation. Path-scoped to the HIGH-risk agentic surface — keep these filters in sync with eval-regression.yml's `paths`.

approver_count.note: Non-author approval: creatorVoteCounts:false means the PR author's own vote does not satisfy the requirement — the Azure analogue of GitHub's required non-author approval. On a single-maintainer repo, see RAILS.md 'Solo-repo accommodation' (bypass via the 'Bypass policies when completing pull requests' permission).

required_reviewers.message: Code-owner review required on gated paths (the CODEOWNERS analogue). Keep in sync with the <<GATED_PATHS>> regex in security.yml and the security rubric.
required_reviewers.note: <<CODE_OWNER>> — a user or group descriptor/email. Azure required-reviewer policy is the CODEOWNERS analogue; path filters mirror the gated-path regex.
